tagline: What's new since you last opened Agent Code — built so designers and tech folks both get what shipped.

highlights.text: This very panel — patch notes on update

sections.title: Patch notes on update
sections.subtitle: You're looking at it. Every release now opens a panel like this with what changed.
sections.body:
When you update Agent Code, this panel pops up once with the highlights of every version you skipped. Each entry is written to be useful **both for designers and tech folks** — the *why* in plain language, the *how* tucked behind a collapsible block for whoever wants to dig in.

You can turn it off anytime with the toggle at the bottom, or reopen it from the Command Palette: **Agent Code: What's New**.
sections.techDetails: Changelogs live as one JSON per version under `media/changelogs/<version>.json`. The host loads every file with `version > lastSeenVersion && version <= currentVersion` from `globalState`, hands the bundle to the webview, and stores the new high-water mark when you dismiss the panel. The visuals (toast, palette, size chart) are data-driven React components — no PNGs to maintain.

sections.title: Auto-update via GitHub Releases
sections.subtitle: One-click installs. No more chasing the latest .vsix.
sections.body:
When a new version ships, every installed copy checks GitHub Releases every 6 hours and offers you the new package right inside VS Code. Click **Update**, a progress bar streams the download, VS Code installs it, you reload the window. That's the loop.

The check is fail-soft: no network, no toast. Opt out anytime from `agentCode.checkForUpdates`.
sections.techDetails: The extension polls `repos/elyas-tirit/Agent-Code/releases/latest`, parses the semver tag, and compares to `package.json`. Throttle (last-check timestamp) and the per-version skip live in `globalState`. The `.vsix` streams via `fetch` + `Readable.fromWeb` piped through a counting `Transform` into `fs.createWriteStream` — so the progress bar is real and a 70 MB download never allocates 70 MB on the JS heap. Install goes through `workbench.extensions.installExtension` with a `Uri.file`.

visual.title: Agent Code 0.0.4 is available
visual.body: Three changes since your version — including this very panel.

sections.title: Updates are 17× lighter
sections.subtitle: Bundled binaries you didn't need — gone.
sections.body:
Every previous build dragged a ~150 MB platform-specific Claude CLI binary inside the package — useless, because Agent Code uses your globally-installed Claude Code CLI. Dropping those native packages from the `.vsix` shrinks the install (and every future update) to about **9 MB**.

In practice: updates now finish in seconds even on a slow connection, and the install footprint stops eating disk for no reason.
sections.techDetails: Added `node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk-*/**` to `.vscodeignore`. The SDK still works because we pass `pathToClaudeCodeExecutable` explicitly from `resolveClaudePath()` — pointing at the user's installed CLI (`/opt/homebrew/bin/claude`, etc.), never the bundled copy. Also dropped `CLAUDE.md` (internal handoff) and `.github/` from the package.

sections.title: Manual "Check for Updates" command
sections.subtitle: Don't want to wait 6 hours? Trigger the check on demand.
sections.body: Hit **⇧⌘P** → "Agent Code: Check for Updates". The extension queries GitHub right away, bypassing the 6-hour throttle, and either prompts to install or tells you you're already on the latest. Useful when a release just shipped and you don't feel like waiting.
